comment: Both are explicit, named top-level Event categories, both now directly verified; UFO continues DOLCE's lineage (per `continuant-occurrent.md` mapping-004). Not `exact-equivalence-candidate` because UFO's event theory (mereology, causation, modal fragility) is more elaborated than DOLCE's Achievement/Accomplishment split.

Structural mismatch: BFO's Process is temporally extended and sits opposite Process Boundary, not opposite a Stative/Event split. Whether BFO:Process better corresponds to DOLCE:Stative or DOLCE:Event (or neither cleanly) is unresolved.

Both isolate an atomic/instantaneous occurrent, per `note-002`, now on verified primary-source definitions for both sides — but from structurally different hierarchies and not confirmed by a source making this comparison explicitly, so confidence is not raised past medium.
SUMO nests this inside Process rather than treating it as a parallel category; correspondence is exploratory only.

Both isolate an atomic/instantaneous occurrent (per `note-005`, extending `note-002`) — GFO's own text glosses this technical sense as "instantaneous event." Structurally different hierarchies (GFO: subtype of `Change`; DOLCE: subtype of `Event`), and no source draws this comparison itself, so capped at medium like `event-003`.
Same atomic/instantaneous convergence as `event-005`, now against BFO's own Process Boundary sibling class — a three-way convergence per `note-005`.

Both give a positive, named process/event distinction rather than treating "event" as a synonym or umbrella term — YAMATO's "events are made of processes... processes can change, events cannot" parallels DOLCE's cumulative/non-cumulative criterion in spirit, though the two criteria are not formally identical and YAMATO's own OWL is not fetched (quotation-only citation). Not `high` because the underlying formalizations were not directly compared class-for-class.

YAMATO's "events... cannot change" criterion echoes UFO's characterization of events as "modally fragile entities" that "cannot genuinely change... while maintaining their numerical identity" — a substantive terminological and conceptual convergence between two independently-authored sources, not just a shared label.

Structural analog only: `activity_occurrence` is a token/particular occurrence of an `activity` type (explicitly not an instance-of relation, per the source's own NOTE), a different axis than the Process/Event category split found in DOLCE/UFO/YAMATO. Consistent with `object.md`'s TUpper precedent of `unknown`/low confidence.

GUM's own definition text uses the word "events," but only as an informal gloss on its general `Process` class (which also covers non-event "goings-on" more broadly) — an umbrella pattern like BFO/SUMO's rather than a named Event category, per `note-004`.
